tagged_story_for_tts: "Pain isn’t a warning—it’s a currency. Your body doesn’t scream to protect you; it screams because evolution betrays you. Chronic pain rewires your brain faster than cocaine (studies show gray matter loss in 6 months), yet we call it ‘invisible’—as if the agony of nerves firing without cause is a ghost story, not a biological mutiny. The lie? ‘It’ll pass.’ The truth? Pain is the only sense that never adapts. Touch a hot stove once, you learn; live with arthritis, and your nervous system doubles down, amplifying signals like a broken alarm. Doctors prescribe ‘management’—a euphemism for surrender—while Big Pharma profits off the myth that relief is a pill away (opioid deaths: 1M+ since 1999, CDC). Here’s the kicker: Pain isn’t the enemy. Your refusal to name it as a systemic failure is. Ask yourself: If this hurt were visible—blood, fractures, third-degree burns—would you still call it ‘just part of life’?" (599 chars)
